name: markdown-resume-suite
description: Write your resume in Markdown, and style it with CSS (we have templates). Export it as a PDF that actually looks good.

commands.title: Markdown Resume Suite: Export Resume | PDF

markdown-resume-suite.inputMarkdownHome.markdownDescription:
The path of the directory where your resume's Markdown file lives (without trailing slash).

If empty, defaults to the directory of the currently-open file when a relevant command is called.

markdown-resume-suite.inputMarkdownFilen.markdownDescription:
The filename of your resume's Markdown file (with the `.md` extension). 

 Only applicable if `#markdown-resume-suite.inputMarkdownHome#` is not empty.

 If empty, defaults to "resume.md".

markdown-resume-suite.outputHome.markdownDescription:
The path of the directory where you wish your exported resume to be created (without trailing slash). 

 If empty, defaults to the directory of the currently-open file when a relevant command is called.

markdown-resume-suite.outputFilename.markdownDescription:
The filename under which you wish your exported resume to be created (without any given extension). 

 If empty, defaults to the name of the input Markdown file.

markdown-resume-suite.outputOverwrite.markdownDescription: If true, a newly exported resume will overwrite an existing resume with the same filename.

markdown-resume-suite.generateTemplateHo.markdownDescription:
The path of the directory where resume templates will be created (without trailing slash).

Templates are delivered as folders containing exactly one Markdown file, as well as any any other files/folders used to create the final document (e.g., a CSS style sheet, image files). 

 If empty, defaults to the directory of the currently-open file when a relevant command is called.

scripts.pretest: yarn run compile && yarn run lint
